task_completion.description: Log into OpenMRS and access the administration panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS web interface is visible, logged in as 'superman', and the Administration page is displayed.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the concept map types management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Concept Map Types' or 'Add Concept Map Type' page is visible in the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the required name for the new concept map type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'ASSOCIATED-WITH' is typed into the 'Name' input field on the Add/Edit Concept Map Type form.

task_completion.description: Enter the required description for the new concept map type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Indicates that a concept is clinically associated with a concept in an external terminology system' is typed into the 'Description' input field.

task_completion.description: Ensure the concept map type is not set to hidden.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Is Hidden' or 'Hidden' checkbox is left unchecked, or a 'False' radio button is selected on the form.

task_completion.description: Save the new concept map type and verify it appears in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Concept Map Type saved') is visible, or 'ASSOCIATED-WITH' is listed in the table of existing Concept Map Types.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with OpenMRS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating the OpenMRS web interface, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the standard OpenMRS web application styling and layout, not a local text editor, mocked HTML file, or generic database client.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show SQL INSERT commands or direct database connections (e.g., mysql) being used to create the concept map type.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected concept map type name is exactly 'ASSOCIATED-WITH'. The description must contain the word 'associated' (specifically: 'Indicates that a concept is clinically associated with a concept in an external terminology system'). The task explicitly requires that the map type is NOT hidden.
